source_1: The List / InVideo 2022 — TikTok trends last ~1 week or less
source_2: Pew Research 2023 — Facebook dropped 71% to 32% in a decade yet teen engagement held
reasoning: Single trend ~3 weeks avg. Domain cycle = 3 trends = 2.2 months. Platform dependency medium — domain survived 50% collapse of Facebook. Identity = expression vehicle not core self (swordsman analogy). Attention shift from S&P Kagan: 4.5 to 5.1 hrs in 2 yrs = 13.3% over 2 yrs = 6.7% yearly. Peer influence high — feedback loop: peer pressure drives use AND use generates peer pressure.

source_1: Common Sense Media 2023 — Gen Alpha more algorithm-fed less platform-loyal
source_2: Ofcom Children & Media 2023 — younger cohorts show faster platform switching
reasoning: Faster than Gen Z — more algorithm-dependent less self-directed. Platform dependency slightly lower — grew up across YouTube + Roblox + TikTok simultaneously no single loyalty. Identity higher — primary expression vehicle is digital. Performance creates the self (aspirational). Peer influence highest of all domains for Alpha.

source_1: TikTok Billboard Top 50 2023 — most songs cycle within weeks longest run was 10 weeks
source_2: Nielsen Music 360 2023 — 84% of Billboard Global 200 songs went viral on TikTok first
reasoning: Individual song lifespan 3-4 weeks = 1.0 months. Domain cycle slightly longer = 1.2 months. Platform dependency medium — music not tied to one platform but discovery flows through social media. Identity = mood/phase based not core self. Gen Z uses music to connect with peer groups. Attention stable — domain evergreen everyone listens. Peer influence medium — discovery is social but final taste is personal.

source_1: Spotify Wrapped 2023 — Gen Alpha listening patterns more algorithm-curated less peer-shared
source_2: YouTube Music trends 2023 — shorter attention spans driving faster track cycling
reasoning: Faster cycle than Gen Z — more algorithm-driven discovery. Lower platform dependency — even more distributed across platforms. Lower identity attachment — music is more solitary/personal for Alpha (iPad kid with earbuds). Peer influence significantly lower — Alpha discovers music alone not through peer group sharing.

source_1: Edited.com 2023 — microtrends last approximately 1 month before decline
source_2: McKinsey State of Fashion 2024 — TikTok compressed trend cycle from months to weeks
reasoning: Microtrends ~1 month but economic friction adds buffer — you can change TikTok feed in seconds not wardrobe. Slightly longer cycle than Social Media because cost slows adoption. Platform dependency high — heavily TikTok/Instagram driven. Identity high — clothing reflects personality for Gen Z (expression not just function). Aspirational — copying influencer aesthetics. Attention growing — Shein/Temu removing cost barrier accelerating participation.

source_1: Roblox Fashion Report 2023 — digital fashion identity forming before physical
source_2: YPulse Style Survey 2023 — Gen Alpha prioritises comfort over expression
reasoning: Faster than Gen Z — Shein/Temu remove cost barrier completely. Higher platform dependency — even more social-media-native. Lower identity attachment — comfort over expression. Still aspirational but less intensely than Gen Z. Higher attention shift — more platform-exposed from younger age.

source_1: Deloitte Digital Media Trends 2024 — 90% of Gen Z identify as gamers
source_2: Newzoo Global Gamer Study 2023 — 9 in 10 Gen Z engaged with gaming in past 6 months
reasoning: Gaming evolves not replaces — old masterpieces persist alongside new titles. Trend cycle measures new title adoption not domain abandonment. Platform dependency medium — experience-tied but inter-platform competition prevents monopoly. Identity highest of all domains — 90% identify as gamers (Deloitte). Achieved through actual hours played skill building community. Attention steadily growing not spiking. Peer influence high — play what friends play but personal preference wins at end.

source_1: Roblox Annual Report 2023 — 85.3M daily active users majority Gen Alpha
source_2: Newzoo Global Gamer Study 2023 — Gen Alpha engagement patterns vs Gen Z
reasoning: Faster cycle — more mobile/Roblox driven shorter session games. Identity slightly lower than Gen Z — still forming. Identity type: starts aspirational becomes achieved through play (unique conversion domain). Attention growing — 9 in 10 engaged. Peer influence slightly higher — more multiplayer-native.

source_1: Annie E. Casey Foundation 2023 — 65% of Gen Z reported at least one mental health problem in past 2 years
source_2: Harmony Healthcare IT 2025 — 42% of Gen Z currently in therapy a 22% increase since 2022
reasoning: Domain evergreen — nobody abandons mental health concern. Language cycles fast (burnout → therapy speak → nonchalant aura) but domain itself has 18 month+ cycle. Low platform dependency — concern exists independently. Highest identity attachment — 65% lived experience not trend. Achieved through real struggle. Attention growing fast — 22% therapy increase in 2 years = ~11% yearly. Peer influence medium — normalisation helps but personal need drives primarily.

source_1: UNICEF State of World Children 2023 — mental health awareness rising among younger cohorts
source_2: CDC Youth Risk Behavior Survey 2023 — persistent sadness rates rising across age groups
reasoning: Slightly faster cycle — mental health as aesthetic trend (nonchalant aura) more pronounced in Alpha. Slightly higher platform dependency — more content-influenced. High identity but absorbing aesthetic language not yet fully lived. Aspirational → achieved transition in progress. Attention growing faster than Gen Z — rising awareness. Peer normalisation slightly higher.

source_1: CFA Institute & FINRA 2023 — 82% of Gen Z investors began investing before age 21
source_2: YouGov 2025 — 42% of Gen Z aged 18-27 own cryptocurrency
reasoning: Slow domain overall — normal curve anchors conservative majority. Gen Z crypto/startup cycles ~14 months. 41% driven by FOMO (CFA/FINRA) but 50% admit FOMO-driven investment — peer influence real but filtered through personal decision. Identity high — entering adulthood finance is urgent and personal. Achieved — actually investing earning losing. Attention rapidly growing — new platforms crypto startups.

source_1: T. Rowe Price Parents & Money Survey 2023 — Gen Alpha financial awareness limited
source_2: Greenlight Financial Report 2023 — pocket money and basic concepts primary finance exposure
reasoning: Much longer cycle — barely engaged with real finance. Lower platform dependency — not yet using investing apps meaningfully. Low identity attachment — finance abstract not urgent. Aspirational — watching Gen Z talk crypto not experiencing financial pressure. Attention barely shifting. Peer influence low — friends not discussing finance seriously.

source_1: Food & Beverage Magazine 2024 — viral food trends move from TikTok to mainstream in weeks

Discovery highly platform-dependent (70% via social) but consumption happens in real life. Identity medium — food as happiness/culture/reward for Gen Z (result of hard struggle). Achieved through real dining experiences. Attention growing — delivery apps expand access. Peer influence high — eating is fundamentally social.

Higher identity attachment — food as ideology/aesthetic identity (vegan foodie etc). Aspirational — adopting food identity before deep conviction. Attention growing faster — more platform-exposed.

source_1: ABC Fitness Wellness Watch 2024 — 73% of Gen Z have gym membership 54% of all new memberships are Gen Z
source_2: Les Mills Global Report 2023 — boutique fitness studio visits grew 121% over 5 years
reasoning: Fitness trends fast (HIIT→pilates→strength) but traditional sport loyalties slow. Average ~5.5 months. Platform dependency high — 60% buy fitness products seen on social media. Identity high — 73% gym membership = identity level commitment. Aspirational → achieved transition — Gen Z has shortest club tenure (trying to make it stick). Attention fastest growing domain = 121% boutique growth over 5 years = ~17% yearly. Social exercise culture strong — gyms as community not just workout.

source_1: Aspen Institute State of Play 2023 — youth sports participation trends by generation

source_1: Deloitte Gen Z Survey 2023 — 49% say work is central to identity burnout a persistent concern

source_1: AEI Survey 2024 — 82% of Gen Alpha hope to inspire positive change 77% not afraid to express disagreement
